channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Schumer accuses Trump of 'skipping town' during shutdown as president heads to Asia for trade talks
item.description: Sen. Chuck Schumer blasts President Donald Trump for leaving the United States for Asia during the government shutdown, accusing the president of "skipping town.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poway Councilmember Tony Blain charged with four felonies days before recall election
item.description: The San Diego County District Attorney’s office has filed five charges, including four felonies, against embattled Poway City Councilmember Tony Blain.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Over 1,000 high school students across San Diego County walk out for environment
item.description: More than 1,000 students across 19 high schools in San Diego County participated in a state-wide walkout on Friday, urging lawmakers to pass climate related legislation.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: Polling places open Saturday for in-person voting in two of the year’s most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor’s race and the contest to pick New Jersey’s next governor.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Newsom bails out Planned Parenthood with $140M to keep 100 clinics open after Trump cuts
item.description: California governor Gavin Newsom announces $140 million in state funding to keep 109 Planned Parenthood clinics open amid federal cuts and defunding efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dallas pediatrician sued over providing hormone treatments to teens surrenders medical license
item.description: After Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton sued last year, Dr. May Lau said she is moving to Oregon and doesn't need a Texas license anymore.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Xiomara Moore, KPRC 2

item.title: Eleanor Holmes Norton, an 88-year-old Dem congressional delegate, scammed by phony 'cleaning crew' at DC home
item.description: A Washington, D.C., congressional delegate was targeted in a brazen credit card fraud scheme after suspects posed as maintenance workers to access her home and finances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reagan Foundation becomes the latest US institution drawn into Donald Trump's controversies
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute is drawing a volatile mix of blowback and praise for arguing that a Canadian government advertisement playing in U.S. markets misrepresented the 40th president’s words to blast President Donald Trump’s tariff policies.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The CEO of the Alamo's historic site has resigned after a top Texas Republican criticized her
item.description: The CEO of the nonprofit managing the Alamo resigned after a powerful Republican state official criticized her publicly, suggesting that her views aren't compatible with the history of the Texas shrine. Kate Rogers said in a statement Friday that she had resigned the day before, after Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Intel Democrats press Gabbard on Trump administration boat strikes
item.description: Leading Intelligence Committee Democrats are accusing the Trump administration of leaving them in the dark on strikes in the Caribbean that have killed more than 40 people, demanding information about the intelligence community’s role.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida lawmaker revives E-Verify bill for stronger enforcement
item.description: tate lawmakers kicked off last year with not one but three special sessions on illegal immigration. A key debate then was E-Verify, which has now made its return to the statehouse.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bioasphalt could be the savior of soybean farmers
item.description: Soybean farmers caught in the middle of a trade war could find a new market for their crops in the form of bioasphalt.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon accepts $130 million donation to help pay the military during the government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon confirmed Friday that it has accepted an anonymous $130 million gift to help pay members of the military during the government shutdown, raising ethical questions after President Donald Trump had announced that a friend had offered the gift to defray any shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House responds to reports Trump named new ballroom after himself
item.description: The White House told Fox News Digital that any announcement on a name for President Trump's White House ballroom will come directly from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he's ending trade talks with Canada over TV ads
item.description: The abrupt announcement comes after Trump accused Canada of using a "fake" Ronald Reagan ad to sway U.S. courts, raising the stakes in an already tense trade dispute.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: White House says government shutdown could eliminate next inflation report despite optimistic numbers
item.description: U.S. inflation hits 3% in September, beating economist expectations and sending Wall Street to record highs as investors anticipate Federal Reserve rate cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration seeks to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia
item.description: The Justice Department floated sending Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, marking the administration's latest effort to remove the once-deported man amid its ongoing prosecution of him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security recipients get a 2.8% cost-of-living boost in 2026, average of $56 per month
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration's annual cost-of-living adjustment will go up by 2.8% in 2026, translating to an average increase of more than $56 for retirees every month, agency officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security recipients get a 2.8% cost-of-living boost in 2026
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security cost-of-living increase will go up by 2.8% in 2026, which translates to an average increase of more than $56 for retirees every month, agency officials said Friday. The benefits increase will go into effect for Social Security recipients beginning in January.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Inflation hits 3% in first report since shutdown
item.description: The data comes more than a week late, after the Bureau of Labor called back furloughed employees to finish it.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump gives disaster declarations to Alaska and others but denies Illinois, Vermont and Maryland
item.description: President Donald Trump approved major disaster declarations for Alaska, Nebraska, North Dakota and the Leech Lake Band of Ojibwe late Wednesday, while denying requests from Vermont, Illinois and Maryland and leaving other states still waiting for answers.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump hosts roundtable on admin’s efforts combatting cartels
item.description: Earlier this month, Trump declared the U.S. is now at war with drug cartels deemed by the White House as terrorist groups.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Zohran Mamdani responds to ‘racist attacks’ from opponent
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Democratic nominee for mayor Zohran Mamdani responded on Thursday to his opponent’s controversial remarks about 9/11, calling them “racist attacks.” In an interview with PIX on Politics host Dan Mannarino, Mamdani took an emotional tone, addressing former Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy ‘can’t guarantee’ on-time flights during shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy warned Thursday of possible flight delays and cancellations during the government shutdown, saying the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay. “I can’t guarantee you that your flight’s going to be on time.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams endorses Andrew Cuomo
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed independent candidate former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo as his successor on Thursday. During Wednesday night’s final mayoral debate, Cuomo was the only candidate who said he’d accept an endorsement from Adams when asked by moderators.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: Texans split on Trump's immigration policies
item.description: A new poll released Wednesday finds registered voters in Texas are split on immigration policies implemented under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: California leaders write letter to Hegseth, demand answers for artillery fire over I-5
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (FOX 5/KUSI) -- California Democrats sent a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday regarding shrapnel that struck California Highway Patrol (CHP) vehicles during Vice President JD Vance’s visit to Camp Pendleton.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: George Santos says he's 'looking into' repaying fraud victims
item.description: Santos was ordered to pay more than $370,000 in restitution, but the president's clemency order wiped the debt clean.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota cities can't regulate guns. St. Paul is introducing an assault weapon ban anyway
item.description: St. Paul city leaders are expected introduce a gun ordinance Wednesday, as part of an ongoing push from several Minnesota cities to get the state to repeal a law that prevents stricter municipal gun laws.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Tribes struggle as shutdown continues
item.description: The United States is 20 days into the third-longest federal government shutdown in history and Indian Country is bracing for an unstable future ahead as reserve funding for many tribes could begin to dry up.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Judge agrees to extend block on Guard deployment in Chicago while awaiting Supreme Court ruling
item.description: As the U.S. Supreme Court weighs whether to clear the way for the National Guard in Chicago, a federal judge on Wednesday agreed to extend a two-week block on Guard deployment in the Chicago area by 30 days.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Newsom deploys National Guard to support food banks amid federal shutdown
item.description: (FOX 5/KUSI) -- As the government shutdown reached its 22nd day, California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced on Wednesday he will deploy National Guard troops to support food banks throughout the state.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sets record for python removal with help of new partner
item.description: Governor Ron Desantis shared the spotlight Tuesday with an 11-foot Burmese python, making it clear the fight to protect the Everglades from invasive species only continues.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House starts demolishing part of the East Wing
item.description: Dramatic photos show construction equipment tearing into the East Wing façade and windows, though the federal agency that oversees such projects has not approved President Trump's 90,000-square-foot, $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China hits the U.S. back in trade war
item.description: China has expanded its export rules on rare earths, requiring foreign firms to get approval for exporting products containing even small amounts of China-originated materials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces tariffs and an end to U.S. aid to Colombia amid clash over drug trade
item.description: The U.S. will slash assistance to Colombia and enact tariffs on its exports because the country’s leader, Gustavo Petro, “does nothing to stop” drug production, President Donald Trump said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces end to U.S. aid to Colombia
item.description: President Donald Trump said Sunday he would slash U.S. funding to Colombia because the country’s leader “does nothing to stop” drug production.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
